Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to group a list of 18 hospital process events into distinct cases patient journeys There are no explicit Case IDs provided I need to use the attributes timestamp activity resource department patient age patient gender additional info to link events belonging to the same patient

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Structure Each line is an event Key attributes for grouping patient age patient gender department sometimes activity sequence and timestamp logical flow Constraint A single patient journey usually follows a logical flow Register Triage Consult Treatment Discharge Observation The timestamps are interleaved meaning multiple patients are being processed simultaneously

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Refining the Output I need to present this clearly I should assign arbitrary Case IDs eg Case 1 Case 2 Case 3 or descriptive IDs based on the patient I should list the events belonging to each case in chronological order I should explain the reasoning matching demographics and logical process flow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Double Check Did I miss any events Total 18 events Case A 1 4 7 12 15 5 events Case B 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 7 events Case C 3 6 9 13 16 18 6 events 5 plus 7 plus 6 equals 18 All accounted for

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Are there any conflicts Event 12 Consult ER 45M vs Event 7 X ray Radiology 45M In ER processes X rays often happen before or during consultation Timestamp 0815 X ray is before 0823 Consult This makes sense Doctor reviews X ray to diagnose Event 6 Consult Surgery 28M vs Event 13 Surgery Surgery 28M Consult 0813 before Surgery 0825 Makes sense Event 8 Lab Request 0816 vs Event 10 Lab Complete 0820 Makes sense Everything looks consistent
